pr.title: feat(hooks): parent-only-orchestrator dispatch block (closes #154)
pr.summary: New PreToolUse:Agent hook that denies Agent dispatches whose prompt asks the subagent to act as a documented parent-only orchestrator (coverage-expansion, onboarding, app-wide bug-discovery). The Agent / Task tool is parent-only in this environment — a subagent that tries to fan out hits a hard wall and burns a full subagent budget for nothing. The hook catches this statically at the dispatch boundary. Closes issue #154; cited from coverage-expansion/SKILL.md §"Recursive dispatch is impossible", onboarding/SKILL.md §"Pipeline", and bug-discovery/SKILL.md §"Invocation scope — standalone vs journey-scoped". Internal-only — no public API surface added on Steps / ElementAction / matcher tree.

design.argumentOrderConventionReason: PR is a harness-hook + skill-citation change. No element-interactions src/ public API added; the (elementName, pageName, ...) convention does not apply.
design.asyncEverywhereReason: Bash-script hook + skill-doc edits — no JavaScript/TypeScript public API surface; async semantics do not apply.
design.stepsKeptLightweightReason: No additions to Steps; PR adds an enforcement hook + skill citations only.
design.namingConventionReason: No new public methods named; the hook follows the existing `<topic>-<scope>-block.sh` filename pattern from sibling guards.
design.presenceDetectInActionsReason: No new action methods; PR is hook + docs only.
design.webOnlyCastAtSiteReason: No element-acquisition code touched; the cast-at-site rule does not apply.
design.loggingPresentReason: Hook deny messages are emitted via the canonical emit_deny helper (PreToolUse JSON envelope). The user-facing logging categories (tester:navigate / interact / verify / extract / wait / email) are scoped to the runtime Steps API, not to harness hooks.
design.typescriptDisciplineReason: Hook is bash, skill files are markdown — no TypeScript src/ touched.

tests.exerciseRealVueAppReason: Hook tests are pipe-fixture tests that synthesise PreToolUse:Agent payloads and assert the hook's stdout shape. The Vue test app applies to runtime Steps API tests, which this PR does not modify.

build.knownFailures: Hook test suite green at 32/32 on this hook's case file (29 baseline + 3 PR #162 review-regression cases). Full suite green per `bash hooks/tests/run.sh`. No runtime spec changes.

coverage.apiCoverageGate100Reason: PR adds no public API on Steps / ElementAction / matcher tree — the `npx test-coverage` 100% gate is a no-op for this PR's diff.

docs.readmeUpdatedReason: No public API surface added; README §"🛠️ API Reference: Steps" does not need updating per Rule 19's internal-only carve-out.
docs.apiReferenceUpdatedReason: Same as above — the canonical api-reference.md applies to public Steps / ElementAction / matcher methods. PR is hook + skill citations only.
docs.skillFilesUpdatedReason: skills/coverage-expansion/SKILL.md cites the hook in §"Recursive dispatch is impossible". skills/onboarding/SKILL.md adds the parent-only-orchestrator paragraph + hook citation in §"Pipeline". skills/bug-discovery/SKILL.md adds the equivalent citation in §"Invocation scope — standalone vs journey-scoped". scripts/postinstall.js HOOK_MANIFEST registers the new hook under PreToolUse:Agent.

version.patchBumpedOnceReason: Per the user-directive 'no version bumps unless the user explicitly says so' (2026-05-06): this PR resets package.json + package-lock.json to main's 0.3.6 and does not bump. The original PR commit `945a8f1` had bumped 0.3.6 → 0.3.10 under the prior 'one bump per PR' rule (Rule 15); the review-fix commit reverts that bump. Versioning is now release-time, not per-PR. A follow-up PR will rewrite Rule 15 in contributing-to-element-interactions/SKILL.md and add a `version-bump-authorisation-guard.sh` hook that denies `npm version <X>` invocations without explicit user authorisation.
